channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Captured Ukrainians convicted on terrorism charges by Russia in what Kyiv called a sham trial
item.description: Russia on Wednesday convicted 23 captured Ukrainians on terrorism charges stemming from the war in Ukraine in a military court trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinians chanted against Hamas during anti-war protests in the Gaza Strip, witnesses said Wednesday, in a rare show of public anger against the militant group that has long repressed dissent and still rules the territory 17 months into the war with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli military orders evacuation of parts of Gaza City
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas. Israel has vowed to increase military pressure until the militants return the remaining 59 hostages — 24 of whom are believed to be alive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German authorities raid group that allegedly aims to overthrow Eritrean government
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Police were executing raids in six German states Wednesday against a group that authorities say “is part of an international network whose aim is to overthrow the government in Eritrea.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Perpetual adoration is a growing Catholic trend. A Holy Year event is scheduled for the weekend
item.description: Growing numbers of Catholics are taking part in Eucharistic adoration – which this year’s Jubilee will mark this weekend with “24 hours for the Lord.”.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Accuser tells a trial that actor Gérard Depardieu groped her bottom and breasts on film set
item.description: A woman accusing French actor Gérard Depardieu of sexual assault has told a landmark trial in Paris that he groped her buttocks and her breasts several times in three separate incidents on a film set.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine ready to observe ceasefire against energy targets following US-brokered deal
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine is ready to proceed with a ceasefire prohibiting attacks on energy infrastructure, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, in line with a deal that was brokered by the United States during three days of negotiations with Ukrainian and Russian officials in the Saudi capital.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Change in itinerary for US Vice President JD Vance brings cautious relief for Greenland and Denmark
item.description: Greenland and Denmark appear cautiously relieved by the news that U.S. Vice President JD Vance and his wife are changing their itinerary for their visit to Greenland Friday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And David Keyton, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Niger’s junta leader cements his grip on power as he is sworn in as president
item.description: Niger’s junta leader, Abdourahamane Tchiani, has been sworn in as the country’s president for a transition period of five years under a new charter that replaces the West African nation’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: Dalatou Mamane, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deeply unpopular Peruvian president calls for general elections in April 2026
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peruvian President Dina Boluarte said Tuesday the country will hold general elections one year from now in an effort to end years of instability. The deeply unpopular Boularte said the April 2026 polls will elect a new president, 130 deputies and 60 senators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai prime minister survives a challenge by rivals who say her father is pulling the strings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra easily survived a no-confidence vote in Parliament on Wednesday, following a two-day debate in which rivals charged that she has mismanaged the country and let her father, a former prime minister, control her administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director says Israeli soldiers beat him after attack by settlers
item.description: HEBRON, West Bank (AP) — Only a few weeks ago, Hamdan Ballal stood on a stage in Los Angeles accepting an Oscar for the film “No Other Land,” a documentary depicting his West Bank village’s struggle against Israel’s occupation.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says US to ease sanctions as part of targeted ceasefire deal with Ukraine
item.description: Editor's note: This article has been updated to more accurately describe the exclusion of Russian banks from the SWIFT system. Russia said Monday that it expects the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Motorcyclist who vanished into sinkhole is found dead following search
item.description: A motorcyclist was found dead Tuesday around 18 hours after he vanished into a massive sinkhole that opened up in Seoul, South Korea’s largest city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a shelter hit by Trump's aid cuts protects LGBTQ migrants in Mexico
item.description: Hundreds of migrants temporary settled at Mexico's southern border gave up on their dream of reaching the U.S. following President Donald Trump's crackdown on migration.
item.dc:creator: María Teresa Hernández, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: 5 lions rescued from the war in Ukraine settle into a new life in England
item.description: SMARDEN, England (AP) — One malnourished lioness had spent her life confined to an apartment. Another was so shell-shocked she could barely walk.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTY WIGGLESWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, UN envoy says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, the top U.N. official in the world’s youngest nation warned on Monday, lamenting the government’s sudden postponement of the latest peace effort.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Brazil apologizes to families of victims of military dictatorship's mass grave
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil’s government on Monday apologized to families of victims of the country’s military dictatorship whose remains could be among those found in a clandestine mass grave 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela-hired lawyers file petition in El Salvador aimed at freeing Venezuelans deported by US
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — Lawyers hired by the Venezuelan government filed a legal action Monday in El Salvador aimed at freeing 238 Venezuelans deported by the United States who are being held in a Salvadoran maximum-security prison.
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth says 'nobody was texting war plans' after group chat breach
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told reporters Monday that “nobody was texting war plans” following news breaking that Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor-in-chief of The Atlantic, gained access to a Signal group chat featuring Trump administration officials talking about plans for an attack against Houthi
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: South Korean PM Han Duck-soo reinstated as acting president after impeachment overturned
item.description: South Korea's Constitutional Court overturned acting President Han Duck-soo's impeachment, reinstating the nation's No. 2 official as acting leader on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US peace talks with Ukraine, Russia get underway in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Discussions between U.S. and Russian delegations aimed at ending the war in Ukraine are underway Monday in Saudi Arabia, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massive Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of US peace talks
item.description: Ukraine says a Russian drone attack killed seven people across the country on Sunday as the U.S. prepares to mediate peace talks in Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela to resume accepting US deportation flights
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has secured approval from Venezuelan President Nicholas Maduro to deport Venezuelan migrants back to the country from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis makes 1st public appearance in five weeks, returns to the Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis made his first public appearance from Rome's Gemelli Hospital, where he was discharged after a five-week stay for life threatening pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis to be released from hospital Sunday after 5 weeks fighting life-threatening pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday after 38 days battling a severe case of pneumonia in both lungs that threatened his life on two occasions and raised the prospect — for now put to rest — of a papal resignation or funeral.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, TRISHA THOMAS and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, after 38 days battling a life-threatening case of pneumonia in both lungs, his doctors said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UK issues travel warning for US
item.description: The United Kingdom updated its advice for travel to the United States on Thursday, warning of harsh consequences for British passport holders who violate U.S. immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Russian drones strike Ukrainian city of Odesa, underlining challenges for even limited truce
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drones pummeled the Ukrainian Black Sea port city of Odesa, injuring three people and sparking massive fires, officials said Friday, an attack that underlined Moscow's intention to pursue aerial strikes even as it agreed to temporarily halt strikes on energy facilities.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
